body_txt: The #1 Dime is a mundane item which is important to someone. Often, this is because it makes them feel safe , it's used as a source of companionship , it's a signifier of friendship , It Was a Gift from someone they value, it's a token sealing a Childhood Marriage Promise , or it reminds them of past tragedies , including lost loved ones. It's bad enough to separate the character from their Dime, but worse can come to pass if it's damaged, lost, or, worst of all, given away . Expect them to shut down or lose their marbles , going on a Roaring Rampage of Revenge against those responsible. Items from older characters' childhoods are regular Dimes, as are Signature Headgear —frequently retrieved in Indy Hat Rolls — Signature Instruments , and Fatal Family Photos . Orphans commonly have Dimes on their person. The Dime of a child or Manchild will probably be a toy. Whatever a Butt-Monkey 's Dime, messing with it is a great way to take 'em for a ride. Compare and contrast with Iconic Item (also has sentimental value, but is unique) and Artifact of Attraction (of interest to many characters, not just one). The Dime could be a Memento MacGuffin , but by and large, the overlap with MacGuffins is minimal. If the item is food, that's either Trademark Favorite Food or (less specifically) Obsessed with Food . If the character falsely believes the item to be the source of a skill or power, it's a Magic Feather . May overlap with Sentimental Shabbiness .
